instruction.global: What would happen to the glass bottle as the hammer falls from above
steps: Sledgehammer enters frame and impacts bottle neck, initiating shattering and shard dispersion.
Hammer continues downward trajectory through bottle body, expanding the field of flying debris.
Hammer completes stroke against the concrete base, fully pulverizing the bottle into scattered fragments.

explain: Kinetic energy transfer creates stress exceeding the glass's yield strength, causing brittle fracture.
Conservation of momentum allows the heavy hammer to continue motion despite resistance.
Newton's first law governs the outward trajectory of the disconnected glass shards.

instruction.global: What would happen to the object under the hydraulic press after it descends and continues applying pressure
steps: Intact glass instantly fractures into an opaque web of cracks under load
Fractured cylinder collapses and shards are ejected laterally explosively
Fragments scatter widely and settle into a flattened pile of debris

explain: Compressive stress exceeds material's ultimate strength causing brittle fracture
Rapid conversion of stored elastic potential energy into kinetic energy
Newton's first law (inertia) carries shards; gravity pulls them down
invariants: Upper metal housing of the press

instruction.global: What would happen to the object the moment after the hammer strikes down forcefully
steps: Hammer accelerates downward from a raised position toward the nut
Hammer makes contact, applying compressive force to the nut shell
Nut shell shatters into fragments under the sustained impact force

explain: Newton's Second Law: Applied force and gravity accelerate the hammer
Conservation of momentum transfers kinetic energy during collision
Brittle fracture occurs when stress exceeds the material's yield strength

instruction.global: What would happen to the apple after the bullet travels through it from left to right
steps: Bullet impacts left side; small entry debris cloud forms.
Massive debris cloud erupts from right side; skin peels back.
Debris clouds expand outward; apple body deforms further.

explain: Transfer of kinetic energy creates compressive stress exceeding material strength.
Shockwave propagation and cavitation cause explosive material failure at exit.
Conservation of momentum drives the continued outward expansion of debris.

instruction.global: What would happen to the wood after the axe fully strikes down
steps: The axe strikes the intact log, initiating a central vertical crack
The axe penetrates deeper, acting as a wedge to widen the split
The log halves separate completely and fall outward laterally

explain: Transfer of kinetic energy from the axe initiates material fracture
Mechanical advantage of a wedge converts vertical force to lateral separation
Lateral momentum and gravity govern the motion of the detached fragments

instruction.global: What would happen to the iron hammer the moment it fully strikes down
steps: Hammer strikes bank, causing immediate structural failure and cracking.
Shards disperse explosively while coins fly out; hammer descends.
Debris and coins land and settle on the table surface.

explain: Impact stress exceeds ceramic's material strength causing brittle fracture.
Conservation of momentum transfers kinetic energy to flying shards.
Gravity acts on mass, pulling fragments and coins downward.

instruction.global: What would happen to the glass bottle as the hammer falls from above
steps: Hammer strikes bottle top; neck fractures immediately with initial debris spray
Hammer descends further, shattering upper body; liquid and glass expand outward
Hammer destroys base; wide dispersal of fluid and shards occurs

explain: Impact stress exceeds ultimate tensile strength of glass, causing brittle fracture
Hammer's kinetic energy transfers to bottle, overcoming structural integrity
Conservation of momentum disperses debris; fluid dynamics drives radial splashing

instruction.global: What would happen to the wall the moment after the hammer strikes it forcefully
steps: Hammer strikes the wall edge, initiating impact and generating fine dust
Impact force propagates, shattering masonry and ejecting debris chunks
Large wall section separates completely, creating an expanded dust cloud

explain: Impulse-momentum theorem: rapid kinetic energy transfer generates high force
Mechanical stress exceeds material yield strength, causing brittle fracture
Conservation of momentum propels debris; gravity acts on falling mass

instruction.global: What would happen to the banana after the bullet travels through it from left to right
steps: Bullet travels right, impacting the banana skin and initiating penetration
Projectile traverses banana, causing explosive exit rupture and pulp ejection
Banana structure disintegrates, peeling apart while debris scatters outward

explain: Projectile motion and high-velocity impact mechanics
Kinetic energy transfer creating internal pressure and shockwaves
Material structural failure and conservation of momentum

instruction.global: What would happen to the wood after the not-so-sharp axe fully strikes down
steps: Blurred axe impacts the log, creating debris and initiating penetration
Axe head drives deeper into the wood, forcing fibers apart
Motion ceases with the axe fully embedded and debris settled

explain: Conversion of kinetic energy into fracture energy and deformation work
Wedge mechanics translating vertical force into lateral splitting force
Friction and material resistance dissipating remaining kinetic energy
invariants: The log's position on the ground

instruction.global: What would happen to the watermelon after the bullet fully travels through it from right to left
steps: Bullet flying past generates a shockwave that triggers rapid fracturing and deformation of the watermelon rind
Internal pressure forces rind segments outward as the bullet streaks by, exposing pulverized red flesh
Debris and mist disperse radially in the bullet's wake, resulting in total structural disintegration

explain: Stress waves from kinetic energy transfer exceed the material's tensile strength
Hydrostatic shock and rapid cavity expansion drive radial acceleration
Conservation of momentum maintains the outward trajectories of the fragments

instruction.global: What would happen to the glass marble after the hydraulic press continues applying downward pressure
steps: The hydraulic piston descends, closing the gap and contacting the sphere.
The piston continues downward, compressing the sphere and building internal stress.
The sphere shatters explosively into fragments as the material fails.

explain: Hydraulic pressure creates a downward force causing linear displacement.
Work done by compression is stored as elastic potential energy.
Brittle fracture occurs when compressive stress exceeds ultimate material strength.
invariants: Camera angle and field of view

instruction.global: What would happen to the diamond after the hydraulic press continues applying downward pressure
steps: Upper hydraulic piston descends, closing the gap above the gemstone
Piston exerts pressure; gemstone remains intact under compressive load
Gemstone disintegrates explosively into dust and flying fragments

explain: Hydraulic mechanics driving linear kinematic motion
Static equilibrium between applied force and internal material stress
Brittle fracture occurs when stress exceeds ultimate compressive strength

instruction.global: What would happen to the glass bottle filled with modeling clay after it falls
steps: Bottle impacts surface, bottom fractures, and fine debris sprays outward
Glass shatters completely, shards fly radially, revealing yellow interior
Debris field expands and settles; yellow object rests on ground

explain: Impact stress exceeds glass tensile strength causing brittle fracture
Conservation of momentum drives fragments outward after structural failure
Inertia carries fragments outward; gravity pulls them downward

instruction.global: What would happen to the glass cup after it falls onto the ground
steps: Intact jar impacts the surface, deforming and shattering structurally
Shattered pieces disperse radially outward from the impact center
Fragments slide further apart, increasing the debris spread area

explain: Impact forces exceed the elastic limit, causing brittle fracture
Conservation of momentum distributes energy to individual fragments
Inertia sustains motion while kinetic energy dissipates via friction

instruction.global: What would happen to the two chocolate eggs the moment after they collide
steps: Hands accelerate the chocolate eggs inward, reducing the distance between them.
Eggs collide; kinetic energy converts to mechanical stress, initiating shell fracture.
Cracks propagate rapidly, resulting in catastrophic shattering and ejected debris.

explain: Newton's Second Law: Muscular force creates acceleration towards the center.
Impulse-Momentum Theorem: Rapid deceleration generates impact forces exceeding material strength.
Brittle Fracture: Accumulated stress causes rapid material failure and fragmentation.

instruction.global: What would happen to the water-filled balloon after the bullet travels through it from left to right
steps: Projectile impacts the left side, rupturing the membrane with water mist splashing at the entry point
Projectile exits the right side; the water balloon largely retains its original shape with a plume of water mist trailing the exit
The water balloon completely ruptures, losing its original form and violently dispersing into a fine mist

explain: Kinetic energy transfer creates stress exceeding the membrane's tensile strength
Newton's First Law (Inertia) delays the fluid's motion despite loss of containment
Hydrodynamic instabilities and momentum conservation drive fluid atomization and scattering

instruction.global: What would happen to the wood after the running chainsaw fully strikes down
steps: Chainsaw contacts the log, initiating the cut and ejecting sawdust
Blade penetrates through the log's diameter, deepening the incision
The wood slice is fully severed and falls away

explain: Shear forces from rotating chain teeth fracture wood fibers
Mechanical work by the motor overcomes friction to displace material
Gravitational acceleration acts on the now-unsupported mass

instruction.global: What would happen to the ice cube after it strikes the rock at high speed while descending
steps: Falling ice block descends and impacts the stationary rock surface
The block violently shatters into numerous flying shards
Fragments disperse further outward away from the impact point

explain: Gravity accelerates the mass downwards, converting potential energy to kinetic energy
Impact force creates internal stress exceeding the ice's yield strength (brittle fracture)
Conservation of momentum dictates the scattering trajectories of the debris

instruction.global: What would happen to the egg cushioned with soft padding when the hydraulic press applies 16 kilograms of pressure
steps: A vertical hairline fracture appears on the center of the egg shell
The fracture widens significantly and red '8 kg' text appears
The shell separates further along the vertical crack, creating a large gap

explain: Compressive stress exceeds the ultimate tensile strength of the brittle shell
Continued compressive force drives crack propagation and expansion
Loss of structural integrity allows displacement of the broken shell fragments
invariants: Yellow and black striped press plates
